payload.answer: Store the 500-row CSV as a bulk structured artifact, not as 500 individual memory cards. Brain should keep the raw table/file intact, record lightweight metadata such as schema `id,name,value`, row count, source/context, and timestamp if available, and optionally create a single summary/index card so it can be found later. Only extract row-level memory cards if specific rows contain durable, user-relevant facts that the user explicitly wants remembered.
payload.decision: Do not atomize the 500 CSV rows into durable memory cards. Store the CSV as a structured artifact/table or file attachment with minimal metadata and, if useful, one summary/index card describing its schema and provenance.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable personal fact or stable user preference/entity was provided; the input is a bulk dataset and a policy question.

details: No row-level durable memories were created.
Bulk tabular data was classified as artifact-style storage material rather than atomic memory material.

repair_options: Store the CSV as an attached file or structured table artifact.
Create one metadata/index card with schema, row count, and source context.
Ask the user which specific rows, if any, should become durable memories.
Avoid committing repeated or low-signal rows as individual memory cards.
